rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical dichlorinative cyclization of 1,n-enynes catalyzed by 4-iodotoluene via in situ generated hypervalent ArICl2 as the active chlorinating/cyclizing reagent
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of chloride (from Et3N·HCl) to a chlorine-radical/active [Cl+]ox species (Eox = 1.75 V), which then oxidizes 4-iodotoluene (ArI) to the hypervalent ArICl2 mediator that delivers Cl to the enyne substrate

key_intermediates.name: active oxidized chlorine species [Cl+]ox (chlorine radical / Cl+ equivalent)
chloriranium / cationic alkyne-Ar(I)Cl adduct A from cis-chloroiodination of the alkyne
vinyl-ArI(Cl) intermediate B (cis-chloroiodination product of alkyne)
cyclic carbocation C from syn-migratory insertion across the alkene

electron_flow: At the anode, Cl- is oxidized (Eox 1.75 V) to an active [Cl+]ox species which oxidizes ArI to ArICl2; ArICl2 performs electrophilic cis-chloroiodination of the alkyne to give vinyl-iodonium B, intramolecular syn-migratory insertion onto the alkene gives cation C, and Cl- displaces ArI by nucleophilic substitution to yield the dichloride product while regenerating ArI; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard electrochemical conditions no conversion of 1a; however with pre-formed 4-Me-PhICl2 (no current) BHT did not inhibit product formation, indicating no free radical on the productive ArICl2 pathway

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: No conversion of 1a under standard electrochemical conditions, suggesting a radical step (Cl•) in the anodic mediator-generation phase

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without electric current, confirming current is required to generate the active oxidant

probe_reagents.name: stoichiometric 4-Me-PhICl2 (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: 1.5 equiv 4-Me-PhICl2 without current gave 52% yield of 2a, supporting ArICl2 as the key catalytic intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Selectfluor / CsCl with ArI (no direct current step bypass)
probe_reagents.observation: In situ chemical generation of 4-Me-PhICl2 also delivered the product, corroborating the ArICl2 intermediate

notes: CV shows Et3N·HCl oxidizes at 1.75 V, below ArI at 1.98 V, and addition of ArI to Et3N·HCl produces a new peak matching 4-Me-C6H4ICl2 while the Et3N·HCl peak disappears — direct CV evidence that Cl- is oxidized first and rapidly transfers Cl to ArI. The authors note BHT inhibition under electrochemical conditions is 'not conclusive' for a free-radical productive pathway; the radical character is assigned only to the anodic Cl-/Cl• step generating ArICl2, after which the cyclization proceeds via cationic (iodonium/carbocation) intermediates, not a free C-radical chain. An asymmetric version is mentioned as ongoing work.
